title: container stop: kill conmon
body:
Old versions of conmon have a bug where they create the exit file before
closing open file descriptors causing a race condition when restarting
containers with open ports since we cannot bind the ports as they're not
yet closed by conmon.

Killing the old conmon PID is ~okay since it forces the FDs of old
conmons to be closed, while it's a NOP for newer versions which should
have exited already.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@mheon @baude PTAL
Actually, this should only be in restart - otherwise, the cleanup process won't fire

> @vrothberg You broke windows

Let's merge then! :-D

> Actually, this should only be in restart - otherwise, the cleanup process won't fire

Done. Now using `syscall` instead of `unix`.

```
[+0037s] # github.com/containers/libpod/libpod
[+0037s] libpod/container_internal.go:1169:14: undefined: syscall.Kill
[+0038s] make: *** [Makefile:170: podman-remote-windows] Error 2
```

I don't think you got all of it... I guess Windows' syscall package is missing the define?
